rule: Standard may be exchanged for Move or Swift; Move may be exchanged for Swift; reverse exchanges are not allowed.
currentEvidence: scripts/engine/combat/action/action-engine-v2.js payStandard() can consume Move or Swift; payMove() can consume Swift.

rule: A Full-Round Action sacrifices Standard, Move, and Swift actions for the turn.
currentEvidence: ActionEngine comments and logic say Full-Round consumes Standard + Move while Swift remains available.

rule: Declared tactical states should have one canonical lifecycle for storage, math, display, and expiration.
currentEvidence: States are split across combatStatus flags, ActiveEffects, roll-dialog situational flags, actor.system.actionEconomy, actor.system.actions, combatant.resources, and legacy components.

rule: Fight Defensively gives +2 Reflex, or +5 Reflex if trained in Acrobatics, plus -5 attacks until start of next turn.
currentEvidence: ActiveEffectsManager computes Acrobatics-aware +5, but CombatStatusResolver hardcodes +2 for defensiveMode=fightingDefensively.

rule: Total Defense gives +5 Reflex, or +10 Reflex if trained in Acrobatics, and the character makes no attacks until next turn.
currentEvidence: ActiveEffectsManager computes Acrobatics-aware +10, but CombatStatusResolver hardcodes +5 and attack locking path is not confirmed across attack/reaction paths.

rule: Cover, prone, and defensive mode controls should mutate the canonical combat state.
currentEvidence: combat-status-panel.hbs contains data-action values for cover/defensive/prone controls, but search found no clear sheet event handler matches in the inspected snapshot.

rule: Aim requires two Swift Actions and should affect the next qualifying ranged attack.
currentEvidence: roll-config.js offers an Aiming checkbox and returns situational.aiming, but no durable two-swift aim state/consumption path was confirmed.

rule: Charge is a Standard Action in the supplied skeleton and must carry charge context into attack options; GM adjudicates path legality.
currentEvidence: roll-config.js returns situational.charging while legacy combat-action-bar spends fullRound and creates a custom Charging ActiveEffect.

rule: Bracing an autofire-only weapon takes two Swift Actions immediately before the qualifying attack and reduces Autofire/Burst penalty to -2.
currentEvidence: Braced option logic appears in prior attack-option audit, but no canonical brace state with cost, weapon gating, and expiration was confirmed.

rule: Recover requires three Swift Actions and persistent conditions block recovery.
currentEvidence: ActorEngine.recoverConditionStep() tracks progress using conditionRecoverProgress but does not itself consume canonical v2 swift action economy.

rule: Second Wind normally costs a Swift Action unless feat/rule changes it.
currentEvidence: SecondWindRules checks combatant.resources.swift, ActorEngine.applySecondWind mutates HP/uses but does not spend actions, and callers spend actions through different paths.

rule: Condition Track levels are numeric penalties and Helpless; they should not automatically become unrelated status effects unless a houserule says so.
currentEvidence: houserule-status-effects.js maps condition levels to Fatigued, Exhausted, Stunned, and Helpless.

rule: Current Conditions and Effects should reliably show active effects that are impacting the actor.
currentEvidence: ActorEffectsAggregator catches adapter failures; active-effect-adapter.js appears to call missing private builder methods in the inspected snapshot.

rule: Flat-Footed removes Dexterity bonus to Reflex and restricts free actions until the character acts or state clears.
currentEvidence: No clear canonical flat-footed state lifecycle was found during this pass.

rule: Grabbed, Grappled, and Pinned have different escape rules, penalties, durations, and action locks.
currentEvidence: Phase 0F found a useful grapple skeleton but unsafe rules fidelity; this state audit confirms generic effects are not enough.

rule: Stun damage can create unconscious and condition track outcomes based on HP and original damage threshold comparison.
currentEvidence: Phase 0G found Stun needs damage packet support; this pass did not confirm a canonical stun-to-state bridge.
